rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes causal inferential leaps (ρ=0.71 → 'prompt-driven' → correctness signal) and its penultimate conclusion (ECE=0.058 ≈ ECE=0.041 → calibration maintained) is weak; the final paragraph directly contradicts the diagnostic finding by calling verbalized confidence a 'reliable signal of correctness' after the paper itself concluded it is a difficulty estimate. Logical consistency is central to any substantive review of a scientific argument.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (ECE values, Spearman ρ, bin fractions, 200-problem held-out set) are cited in support of sweeping generalizations (model-agnostic, 7B–70B range, any language); checking whether the reported evidence warrants those conclusions is load-bearing for an evidence-focused reading.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is colloquial phrasing that routinely signals a request for prose-level feedback; sentence and paragraph clarity is plausibly the primary intent and would be load-bearing if the delegation targets writing quality rather than scientific argument.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for feedback on the prose quality and writing clarity, or on the logical soundness and evidence-claim alignment of the scientific argument (or both)?
